title: Evaluate offline routing ML baselines after readiness gate
goal: Only after the registered readiness experiment passes, evaluate simple external ML baselines offline against the deterministic routing heuristic using frozen evidence-bound cohorts, with calibrated abstention and no production effect.

acceptance.assertion: No model training starts unless the Vibe-Lab shadow-readiness experiment has a reviewed result meeting its preregistered completeness, privacy and no-effect gates.
Use established offline ML tooling such as scikit-learn for the baseline rather than implementing a custom ML framework; any additional dependency has an explicit bounded purpose.
At minimum compare an interpretable linear baseline and one tree/boosting-style baseline against the existing deterministic routing heuristic on frozen train/test cohorts.
Evaluation prevents future-evidence, repository and reviewer leakage with a documented split strategy and preserves abstentions and missing labels.
Report calibration, uncertainty or abstention behavior in addition to aggregate accuracy so low-confidence recommendations can remain advisory.
Model outputs remain offline advisory evidence; no automatic routing, task priority, queue, merge, policy or runtime effect is introduced.
Dataset identity, feature contract, model parameters, metrics and artifact hashes are reproducible and reviewable without exporting raw private prompts or transcripts.

current_blockers: OPERATOR-ML-READINESS-V1-T005 must make reviewer-disagreement, test/quarantine provenance and execution-failure provenance measurable before T004 can be meaningfully re-audited
The live eligible treatment cohort currently contains zero cases
